name: gptme
description: Your agent in your terminal, equipped with local tools: writes code, uses the terminal, browses the web. Make your own persistent autonomous agent on top!

commit.message:
fix(tools): prevent content from leaking into unrelated ToolUse parameters (#1646)

* fix(tools): prevent content from leaking into unrelated ToolUse parameters

The `_to_params()` method was unconditionally appending `self.content` to
`self.args` for positional parameter mapping. This caused content to be
incorrectly mapped to unrelated parameters — e.g., the read tool's
`start_line` getting the filename from content.

Now content is only appended as a positional parameter when it fills
exactly the last remaining parameter slot. This preserves correct
behavior for tools like save/patch (where content IS the body parameter)
while fixing read and other tools where content is just display text.

Also fixes the read tool example which incorrectly passed the path as
both args and content.

Fixes #1645

* fix(tools): fix ToolUse repr leaking into screenshot examples + add test

screenshot.py was using ToolUse directly in an f-string without .to_output(),
causing the raw dataclass repr (containing 'ToolUse(...)') to appear in the
system prompt examples. Fix by calling .to_output() like all other tool files.

Also adds test_no_tooluse_repr_in_examples to catch this class of bug across
all tools for all tool formats.

Fixes https://github.com/gptme/gptme/issues/1645 (mentioned by @ErikBjare)

* fix(tools): address Greptile review — use required-param check and callable examples

- Replace `remaining == 1` heuristic in `_to_params()` with a check on
  whether the next parameter slot is `required=True`. This is both more
  correct and more robust:
  - Prevents content leaking into optional params when args partially fill
    a multi-param tool (e.g. read with args=[path, start_line] no longer
    maps content to end_line)
  - Handles tools that gain optional trailing params without silently
    dropping content (e.g. hypothetical save+encoding)
- Make screenshot tool `examples` a callable `(tool_format) -> str` so
  it renders in the correct format (markdown/xml/tool) instead of always
  using the default markdown format set at module load time

* Update gptme/tools/base.py

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tools): add missing args.append(self.content) body to if-block

The if-block added in d374ec401 had no body — Python requires at least
one indented statement. Also removes the redundant `self.content is not None`
inner check (already guaranteed by outer condition on line 769).

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate release pipeline into single workflow (#1643)

* refactor: consolidate scheduled-release.yml into release.yml

Merge the two release workflows into one unified pipeline:

- schedule/dispatch: check → prepare (bump, changelog, create release) → publish
- release:created (manual make release-*): publish directly

This removes the cross-workflow dispatch workaround (GITHUB_TOKEN can't
fire release:created for other workflows). Now the prepare and publish
jobs are in the same workflow, so no dispatch is needed.

Updated CI check exclusion from "Scheduled Release" to "Release" to
match the new workflow name.

* fix(release): anchor CI exclusion regex, pin changelog script to commit

- CI check exclusion: replace broad "Release" substring match with
  exact job names (Pre-flight checks, Create release, Publish release)
  to avoid accidentally hiding future workflows containing "Release".
- Pin build_changelog.py to commit ade74fd (Oct 2025) instead of
  mutable master branch, both in workflow and Makefile.

commit.message:
fix(eval/dspy): handle empty messages gracefully in tool_usage_metric (#1642)

* fix(eval/dspy): handle empty messages gracefully in tool_usage_metric

When API quota exhaustion or other errors cause GptmeModule.forward() to
return a prediction with empty messages, tool_usage_metric would raise
ValueError, crashing the entire evaluation pipeline.

Changes:
- tool_usage_metric: return 0.0 with warning instead of raising on empty messages
- _evaluate_prompt: catch per-example failures and score as 0.0 (quota errors still re-raise)
- Add test for empty-messages graceful degradation

Fixes #1626.

* fix(eval/dspy): use getattr guard for pred.messages robustness

Address Greptile review: use getattr(pred, "messages", None) to
handle bare dspy.Prediction objects without a messages attribute.

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(release): poetry via pipx, upload retry, Docker tag policy (#1641)

* fix(release): poetry via pipx, upload retry, Docker :latest only for stable

- Install poetry via pipx (consistent with other workflows)
- Add retry loop before gh release upload (handles propagation delay)
- Docker :latest tag only pushed for stable releases and master pushes;
  dev/pre-releases get :dev tag instead

* fix(build): fix Windows sidecar build by invoking bash explicitly

On Windows runners, `make` uses cmd.exe as its shell. When it runs
`./scripts/build_server_executable.sh`, Windows routes the .sh file
to WSL instead of Git bash, causing "no installed distributions" error.

Fix by calling `bash` explicitly in the Makefile target.

* fix(tauri): don't block all releases when one sidecar platform fails

The release job requires all build-sidecar matrix jobs to pass. If
Windows sidecar fails, Linux/macOS releases are blocked too even though
they have their own working sidecars.

Add !cancelled() so each platform's release proceeds independently.
A failed sidecar only affects its own platform's release.

* fix(tauri): require lint/test/build success, only tolerate sidecar failures

The previous !cancelled() was too broad — it would run the release job
even if lint or tests failed. Now explicitly check that lint, test, and
build succeeded, while allowing build-sidecar to partially fail.

* fix(build): broaden pre-release detection for Docker tag policy

Also match PEP 440 canonical alpha/beta/RC versions without dot prefix
(e.g. v0.31.1a1, v0.31.1rc1) in addition to dotted format (.dev, .a).

commit.message:
feat(prompts): distinguish agent instruction files from context files (#1556)

Separates AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md files from regular context files
and adds explicit framing that these are user-defined rules that MUST be
followed, not just informational context.

- Agent files now appear under '## Agent Instructions' with clear directive
- Context files remain under '## Selected files' as before
- Helps ensure LLMs respect user-defined workflows and preferences

commit.message:
feat(release): centralize release pipeline in release.yml (#1640)

* feat(release): upload artifacts to GitHub releases and trigger build workflows

The scheduled-release workflow creates releases using GITHUB_TOKEN,
which doesn't trigger other workflows (GitHub limitation). This means
build.yml (Docker + PyInstaller) and tauri.yml never ran for dev releases.

Fix by:
- Upload Python packages (.whl, .tar.gz) to the GH release directly
  in publish_release.sh (always, not just for PyPI publishes)
- Add workflow_dispatch trigger to build.yml and tauri.yml so they
  can be triggered explicitly from the scheduled-release workflow
- Trigger both build.yml and tauri.yml after creating the release
- Upload PyInstaller binaries to the GH release with OS/arch suffix
- Fix Tauri release to use correct tag and set prerelease flag for
  dev releases

* fix: address review feedback

- Checkout tagged ref in all Tauri jobs (lint, test, build), not just
  build-sidecar and release
- Broaden prerelease detection to cover .a, .b, .rc suffixes
- Clean dist/ before poetry build to avoid stale artifacts
- Add shell: bash to PyInstaller upload step for Windows compat

* refactor: make release.yml the central release handler

Restructure the release pipeline so release.yml is the single
"what happens when a release exists" workflow:

- release.yml: triggers on release:created (manual) + workflow_dispatch
  (scheduled). Builds Python packages, uploads to GH release, publishes
  to PyPI, triggers build.yml + tauri.yml.
- scheduled-release.yml: simplified to preflight + bump + changelog +
  create GH release + trigger release.yml. No longer does PyPI/artifacts.
- publish_release.sh: slimmed to just push tag + create GH release.
  Adds idempotency guard for safe re-runs.
- build.yml/tauri.yml: remove push tag triggers (release builds come
  via workflow_dispatch from release.yml now).

Also fixes Greptile review comments:
- releaseDraft only when not dispatched with a tag (prevents unpublishing)
- Tighter glob in PyInstaller rename loop
- Idempotency guard on gh release create

* fix(release): reorder steps so PyPI publish is last, use explicit tag ref

- Move Trigger artifact builds before Publish to PyPI since PyPI
  rejects duplicate uploads (non-idempotent). All prior steps are
  safe to re-run (--clobber / fire-and-forget dispatches).
- Use github.event.release.tag_name as fallback for release:created
  events (more explicit than relying on github.ref_name alone).
- Extract TAG as job-level env var to reduce repetition.

commit.message:
refactor(message): nest token fields under `usage` in MessageMetadata (#1638)

Restructure MessageMetadata to nest token count fields (input_tokens,
output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_creation_tokens) under a `usage`
sub-dict, matching LLM API response structure (both Anthropic and OpenAI
return usage as a nested object). Cost remains a top-level derived field.

Add _migrate_metadata() helper for backward-compatible deserialization
of old flat-format logs, applied in JSONL, TOML, and toml_to_msgs paths.

commit.message:
fix(release): don't block dev releases on flaky API test failures (#1639)

* fix(release): don't block dev releases on flaky API test failures

Two fixes to the scheduled release preflight checks:

1. CI check: Switch from check-suites to check-runs API and exclude
   "API tests" and "Scheduled Release" runs. The previous approach
   checked all check suites, so a single flaky API test failure
   (e.g. anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5) would block the entire release.

2. LAST_TAG detection: Use `git tag --sort=-version:refname` instead
   of `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`. Dev release tags live on
   version-bump commits that aren't pushed to master, so git-describe
   can't find them (it only walks reachable commits). Also use
   merge-base for commit counting to handle detached tag commits.

* fix: log warning when merge-base fails in preflight check

commit.message:
fix(models): proper metadata for openai-subscription models (#1637)

* fix(models): add proper per-model metadata for openai-subscription models

The openai-subscription models were all sharing identical dummy metadata
(128k context, 16k output) via a dict comprehension. Now each model has
its actual specs from the OpenAI API docs:

- gpt-5.4: 1.05M context (was 128k)
- gpt-5.3-codex/5.2/5.2-codex: 400k context, 128k output
- gpt-5.3-codex-spark: 128k context, text-only (no vision)
- gpt-5.1/codex/codex-max: 400k context, 128k output
- gpt-5.1-codex-mini: 400k context, 128k output

Also adds knowledge_cutoff dates, API-equivalent pricing, and moves
definitions to OPENAI_SUBSCRIPTION_MODELS in llm_openai_models.py
(referenced from models.py) for better organization.

* fix stale comment about price override

The comment incorrectly stated that models.py overrides prices to 0.

commit.message:
feat(lessons): support LLM-judge effectiveness state (#1636)

* feat(lessons): support LLM-judge effectiveness state

Teach the hybrid lesson matcher to read judge-derived effectiveness
counters alongside Thompson-sampling posteriors. Judge-only state
files now work, and combined arms average the two signals so #1574
can complement #1573 without changing the existing state-file path.

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

* fix(lessons): address Greptile review — smooth judge scores, rename loader fn

- Add Laplace (add-1) smoothing to _score_from_judge_arm so sparse verdicts
  don't produce extreme 0.0/1.0 scores; mirrors the implicit Beta(1,1) prior
  in the TS arm (fixes scoring instability flagged in Greptile review)
- Rename _load_ts_posteriors → _load_effectiveness_scores since the function
  now loads both TS and judge signals, not just TS posteriors
- Update effectiveness_state_file docstring to document the full arm schema
- Update tests: import name + expected values for smoothed judge scoring

* fix(lessons): address remaining Greptile review — stale TS refs and missing test

- Update stale "TS state" log messages/docstrings to say "effectiveness state"
- Fix _default_effectiveness_state_file and _lesson_lookup_keys docstrings
- Fix inline comment in __init__
- Update _effectiveness_score docstring to document TS/judge/combined paths
- Add test_score_from_judge_arm_returns_none_without_judge_fields (symmetry with existing TS test)

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats rename` command (#1622)

* feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats rename` command

Adds the ability to rename a conversation's display name without
moving files. The conversation ID stays the same — only the display
name in config.toml is updated.

- `rename_conversation()` in logmanager.py
- `gptme-util chats rename <id> <name>` CLI command
- 6 tests covering success, not-found, config preservation, idempotency

* fix(chats-rename): avoid workspace symlink side effect, strengthen test

- Replace ChatConfig.save() call in rename_conversation() with a targeted
  TOML update that only touches the name field. ChatConfig.save() also
  manages the workspace symlink, which would silently create a spurious
  workspace -> cwd symlink for conversations that have no pre-existing
  workspace configuration (Greptile review finding).
- Add test_rename_conversation_no_workspace_symlink to catch regressions.
- Strengthen test_rename_conversation_preserves_existing_config to also
  assert that the original stream field survives the rename (Greptile review).

* test(chats): assert no workspace symlink in preserves-config test

---------

commit.message:
fix(eval/dspy): handle API quota exhaustion gracefully in optimize workflow (#1628)

* fix(eval/dspy): handle API quota exhaustion gracefully in optimize workflow

- Add `_is_quota_error()` helper that detects quota/rate-limit errors from
  Anthropic's BadRequestError ("usage limits"), RateLimitError, and wrapped
  errors from litellm/DSPy by checking the error message
- `GptmeModule.forward()`: re-raise quota/rate-limit errors instead of
  swallowing them as empty-messages predictions (which caused misleading
  ValueError downstream)
- `cli.py` quick-test command: catch quota errors and exit 0 with a warning
  instead of exit 1 — quota exhaustion is infrastructure state, not a
  code regression

Fixes #1627

* fix(eval/dspy): tighten quota error detection and add tests

- Replace bare "quota" phrase with "api quota" in _is_quota_error()
  message-based matching to avoid false positives from unrelated quota
  errors (e.g. "disk quota exceeded" from OS-level errors)
- Add test_is_quota_error() covering positive cases (usage limits, rate
  limit, api quota) and negative cases (disk quota, generic errors)

Addresses Greptile review concern about overly broad phrase matching.

* fix(eval/dspy): address Greptile review feedback

- Rename _is_quota_error -> is_quota_error (was effectively public via cli.py/tests imports)
- Re-raise quota errors in optimize_prompt() broad except block (was silently returning base_prompt)
- Add quota graceful exit to optimize and optimize-gepa CLI commands (consistent UX with quick-test)
- Add isinstance-based test coverage for anthropic.RateLimitError and anthropic.BadRequestError paths

* fix(tests): skip requires_api tests when Anthropic quota is exhausted

At session start, make a minimal API call to detect if the Anthropic
quota is exhausted (400 "usage limits" error). If so, mark all
requires_api tests as SKIPPED instead of letting them fail with
confusing assertion errors downstream.

This fixes CI when the monthly quota runs out mid-cycle: tests that
depend on real API calls now exit cleanly rather than failing on
unrelated assertions (e.g. exit_code != 0, events never arriving).

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shell): do not process backslash escapes inside single quotes (#1624)

* fix(shell): do not process backslash escapes inside single quotes

In bash, single-quoted strings treat backslashes as literal
characters — they do not escape anything. The _find_quotes
function was incorrectly skipping backslash-escaped characters
globally, including inside single quotes. This caused incorrect
quote boundary detection.

For example, in a command like:
  echo 'foo\\' ; dangerous_cmd
The backslash before the closing quote would cause _find_quotes
to skip it, treating "dangerous_cmd" as still inside the quoted
string. Since is_denylisted() skips quoted regions, this could
allow dangerous commands to bypass the denylist.

The fix adds a `not in_single` guard to the backslash escape
handling, matching bash semantics.

* test: add regression test for single-quote backslash bypass

Add a dedicated test to prevent regression of the _find_quotes fix.
This covers the scenario where a backslash before a closing single
quote could cause dangerous commands after the quote to be treated
as quoted content and bypass the denylist.

Signed-off-by: JiangNan <1394485448@qq.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: JiangNan <1394485448@qq.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): skill marketplace — install, uninstall, validate, init, publish (#1566)

* feat(skills): add install/uninstall/validate commands to gptme-util skills

Phase 2 of the skill marketplace (gptme#1551). Adds:

- `gptme-util skills install <source>` — install from registry name, git URL, or local path
- `gptme-util skills uninstall <name>` — remove installed skill
- `gptme-util skills validate <path>` — check SKILL.md format and marketplace metadata
- `gptme-util skills installed` — list installed skills with versions

Installation architecture:
- Skills cloned/copied to ~/.config/gptme/skills/
- Manifest tracked in ~/.config/gptme/installed-skills.yaml
- Git URL support with sparse checkout for efficient cloning
- Registry resolution via gptme-contrib convention
- 20 tests covering install, uninstall, validate, manifest, and edge cases

* feat(skills): add init and publish commands to gptme-util skills

- `gptme-util skills init <path>` scaffolds a new skill with SKILL.md
  template, supporting --name, --description, --author, --tags options
- `gptme-util skills publish <path>` validates, packages as .tar.gz
  archive, and shows submission instructions for gptme-contrib registry
- publish excludes hidden files and __pycache__, shows warnings for
  missing marketplace metadata but still packages
- 15 new tests covering init (6) and publish (9), all passing

* fix(skills): prevent path traversal and symlink attacks in skill installer

Address security issues found in Greptile review:

- Add `_sanitize_skill_name()` using `Path(name).name` to strip traversal
  components (e.g. `../../evil`) from all skill name inputs
- Apply sanitization to git install (temp_name), local install (name from
  SKILL.md frontmatter), post-install name resolution, and uninstall
- Validate that uninstall path is within skills_dir before deletion,
  preventing arbitrary directory deletion via manifest tampering
- Skip symlinks in publish archive to prevent sensitive file leakage
- Guard `_find_skill_md` against `NotADirectoryError` when given a file path

* fix(skills): address all 5 security/logic bugs from Greptile round-2 review

- _sanitize_skill_name: Path('..').name returns '..' unchanged — explicitly
  reject '.' and '..' to prevent path traversal with --force flag
- publish_skill: sanitize skill_name and version from SKILL.md frontmatter
  before constructing archive path (prevents path traversal via crafted name)
- _resolve_registry_skill: validate name format before returning URL, making
  the 'Skill not found in registry' error branch actually reachable (prevents
  60-second git clone timeout on typos/invalid names)
- skills_validate CLI: separate 'recommended' warnings from real errors,
  matching publish_skill behavior (a skill that publishes no longer fails validate)
- init_skill: escape braces in user-supplied values before str.format() to
  prevent KeyError on inputs like "Parses JSON {key} values"

* fix(skills): sanitize user-supplied --name and catch TimeoutExpired in git ops

* fix(skills): preserve symlinks in copytree and replace assert with explicit check

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(eval): add debug-type-error and find-and-fix eval tests

Two new practical eval tests for the basic suite:

- debug-type-error: Agent must diagnose a TypeError from mixed types
  in a JSON config (strings where numbers expected), then fix the config.
  Tests diagnostic reasoning, not just code writing.

- find-and-fix: Agent must find all callers of a deprecated function
  across multiple files and update them to use the replacement.
  Tests multi-file search and coordinated edits.

Both test real-world agent capabilities underrepresented in the
existing suite (10 → 12 tests).

  explanatory comments don't fail the check

commit.message:
feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats stats` command (#1618)

* feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats stats` command

Add conversation statistics to the CLI showing:
- Total conversations and messages
- Average/median messages per conversation
- Date range (oldest to newest)
- Recent activity (last 7/30 days)
- Agent breakdown (interactive vs named agents)
- Daily activity histogram (last 7 days)

Supports `--since` filtering (YYYY-MM-DD or Nd format) for
scoped analysis, and `--json` for machine-readable output.

Also makes tool initialization lazy in the chats group -
commands that don't need tools (like stats) now start instantly
instead of waiting for browser/tool detection.

Includes 13 tests covering parse logic, stats computation,
empty states, filtering, and CLI integration.

* fix(chats-stats): address greptile review feedback

- Fix median calculation for even-length lists using statistics.median()
  (previously returned upper-middle element, not true median)
- Catch ValueError from _parse_since and raise click.UsageError
  (previously showed Python traceback on invalid --since input)
- Hide "Recent Activity" section when --since is set, since the data
  is already scoped to the requested window (values would equal total)
- Add tests for even-length median and invalid --since error handling

* fix(chats): use modified ts for display when filtering by --since; drop unused @patch

When --since is active, the filter criterion is conv.modified. Using
conv.created for oldest_ts and daily_counts produced a confusing UX:
"Oldest" could show a date well before the requested window, and the
histogram buckets wouldn't align with the counted conversations.

Now ts_for_display = conv.modified when since_ts is set, so the
"Oldest" date and daily histogram always reflect the same time
dimension as the filter.

Also removes the spurious @patch decorator from test_stats_invalid_since —
_parse_since raises ValueError before get_user_conversations is ever called,
so the mock was never exercised.

* fix(chats): make histogram window consistent between JSON and text output

- Compute hist_days from --since window (or default 14 days)
- Use hist_days for both JSON by_day[:hist_days] and text range(hist_days)
- Dynamic label: 'Daily Activity (last N days)' reflects actual window

Fixes UX inconsistency flagged by Greptile: JSON returned 14 days while
text always showed 7 days, and the label was hardcoded regardless of
the --since value.

* fix(chats): address final Greptile findings

- Remove [dependency-groups] section from pyproject.toml — conflicts with
  Poetry's pytest = "^8.0" declaration (different pytest major versions
  depending on tool used)
- Cap hist_days at 365 to prevent thousands of output lines when --since
  specifies a far-past date like 2020-01-01
- JSON by_day now fills zeros for days with no activity, matching the
  text histogram behavior (both modes now show same window with same data)
- Add 2 tests: test_stats_json_by_day_includes_zeros, test_stats_hist_days_capped_at_365

* fix(chats): fix off-by-one in hist_days and make 365-cap test meaningful

- Remove +1 from hist_days so --since 7d shows 'last 7 days' not 'last 8 days'
- Fix test_stats_hist_days_capped_at_365 to use non-empty mock so hist_days
  is actually computed and the 365 cap is verified via by_day dict length

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add tool exclusion with '-' prefix syntax (#1616)

* feat(cli): add tool exclusion with '-' prefix syntax

Add support for excluding tools from defaults using '-' prefix:
  gptme -t=-browser "summarize code"     # all defaults except browser
  gptme -t=-browser,-tmux "do stuff"     # exclude multiple tools

This complements the existing '+' prefix for additive mode:
  gptme -t +subagent "plan a refactor"   # add subagent to defaults

Mixing '+' and '-' prefixes raises a clear error.

Changes:
- CommaSeparatedChoice: support multiple prefixes via allow_prefixes param
- CLI main.py: parse '-tool' specs into exclusion mode string
- config.py: handle '-' prefixed allowlist by removing from defaults
- Tests: 3 new tests covering CommaSeparatedChoice, single and multi exclusion

* fix(cli): raise error when mixing bare tool names with '-' exclusion syntax

Guard against the silent bug where 'gptme -t=shell,-browser' would
incorrectly enter exclusion mode for all entries, treating 'shell' as
an exclusion too. Now raises a clear UsageError.

Also fix a misleading comment in test_config.py that referenced
'complete' tool subtraction which wasn't actually happening in the code.

* fix(test): use valid tool name in test_tool_exclusion_mixed_bare_and_minus_raises

'browser' is not in _available_tools, so CommaSeparatedChoice rejected it
with 'invalid choice' before the mixing guard in main() was reached.
Use 'tmux' (a valid tool) so the test correctly exercises the mixing guard.

* fix(cli): address greptile review — test assertions and non-default exclusion warning

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats clean` command (#1620)

* feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats clean` command

Add a command to find and remove empty or trivial conversations.
With 24K+ conversations accumulating on agent VMs, managing log
storage becomes important.

Features:
- Finds conversations with 0-1 messages (configurable with -n)
- Shows disk usage per conversation
- Dry-run by default (safe to run)
- --delete flag to actually remove conversations
- --include-test to include test/eval conversations
- --json for machine-readable output

Also refactors the chats CLI group to use lazy tool initialization
(moved from group-level to per-command), so `chats clean` starts
instantly without loading browser/tool dependencies.

* fix(chats-clean): address Greptile review bugs

- Fix JSON output: run deletions before emitting JSON, so
  deleted/freed_bytes reflect actual results not predictions
- Fix freed-bytes accounting: sum only successfully deleted
  conversation sizes instead of total_size
- Fix include_test default: align function default (False) with
  CLI flag default (--include-test is off by default)

* fix(chats-clean): consistent JSON schema for empty results

The no-results JSON path was missing `total_bytes` and `conversations`
fields that the normal path includes. JSON consumers now get the same
schema regardless of whether results were found.

Added test to verify schema consistency.

* fix(chats-clean): address Greptile re-review findings

- Add _ensure_tools() to chats_export (regression from refactor)
- Handle PermissionError in deletion loops to avoid mid-run crash
- Skip conversations with branch history in find_empty_conversations
  (conv.messages only counts main branch; conv.branches > 1 indicates
  meaningful history that shouldn't be flagged as empty)

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats export` command (#1619)

* feat(cli): add `gptme-util chats export` command

Add a CLI command to export conversations to HTML or markdown files.
Previously, exporting was only available via the interactive `/export`
command. This makes it accessible from scripts and automation.

Features:
- `gptme-util chats export <id>` — exports to markdown by default
- `--format html|markdown` — choose output format
- `--output <path>` — custom output file path
- Markdown export skips hidden system messages

- Remove redundant isinstance check (Log.messages is typed list[Message])
- Fix misleading comment: 'Skip hidden system messages' → 'Skip hidden messages'
- Fix double blank line in title (embedded \n + join separator = 2 newlines)
- Remove unused Message import
- Add CLI test for HTML export format to cover template-reading code path

feat(doctor): add lesson health checks to agent doctor (#1613)

* feat(doctor): add lesson health checks to agent doctor

Adds a check_lessons() function to the agent workspace doctor that:
- Scans lesson directories (from gptme.toml [lessons] dirs or default)
- Counts total lessons across all configured directories
- Detects lessons missing YAML frontmatter
- Detects lessons without matching config (no keywords/name/globs)
- Warns about oversized lessons (>100 lines)
- Reports YAML parse errors
- Accepts both lesson format (keywords) and skill format (name)

Includes 9 tests covering all scenarios.

* fix(doctor): address review feedback — skills check cmd + bug fixes

- Add `gptme-util skills check` subcommand that reuses check_lessons()
  (addresses Erik's request to share helper with gptme-util skills)
- Fix double-counting when lesson dirs overlap (e.g. lessons/ + lessons/tools/):

commit.message:
feat(server): add ACP subprocess health monitoring and orphan cleanup (#1583)

* feat(server): add ACP subprocess health monitoring and orphan cleanup

- Add is_subprocess_alive() and process_pid to AcpSessionRuntime for
  subprocess health checking
- Add background health monitor thread that runs every 30s:
  - Calls clean_inactive_sessions() (was never called before)
  - Detects dead ACP subprocesses and removes their sessions
  - Skips sessions that are actively generating
- Add graceful shutdown via atexit that closes all ACP runtimes
- Improve _close_acp_runtime_bg with error handling and PID logging
- Wire health monitor into create_app() server startup
- Add 4 tests covering subprocess liveness, dead process cleanup,
  generating session protection, and monitor start/stop lifecycle

Part of the process-per-session architecture (Phase 3 hardening).

* fix(server): address three Greptile-identified bugs in ACP health monitor

- Duplicate atexit: guard with _health_monitor_atexit_registered flag so
  stop→start cycles don't accumulate atexit handlers
- TOCTOU race: re-check session.generating immediately before remove_session()
  to narrow the window where a new /step could start on a session being evicted
- asyncio.run() in atexit: add AcpSessionRuntime.terminate_subprocess_sync()
  that uses proc.terminate()/kill() synchronously; use it in
  _cleanup_all_acp_sessions() instead of asyncio.run(close()) which is
  unreliable during interpreter shutdown

* fix(server): address second-round Greptile issues in ACP health monitor

Three fixes from Greptile's second review pass:

1. Lazy-start health monitor (api.py + api_v2_sessions.py)
   - Remove unconditional start_acp_health_monitor() from create_app()
   - Instead start lazily on first ACP session creation in api_conversation_step()
   - Avoids background thread + global session-eviction side-effects when ACP
     is never actually used in a deployment

2. _cleanup_all_acp_sessions() now removes sessions from SessionManager
   - Add SessionManager.remove_session(session_id) in finally block
   - Prevents zombie sessions with stale acp_runtime refs persisting after shutdown
   - Safe in atexit path; also correct for hypothetical non-atexit stop

3. terminate_subprocess_sync() clears _client/_session_id after termination
   - Add self._client = None; self._session_id = None in finally block
   - Mirrors what close() does; leaves runtime in consistent state
   - Prevents accidental reuse of stale references

* fix(server): address third-round Greptile issues in ACP health monitor

- Reap zombie process: add proc.wait(timeout=1.0) after proc.kill() in
  terminate_subprocess_sync() so the child's exit status is collected
  instead of accumulating as a zombie in the process table
- Thread-safety: wrap entire check-then-create in start_acp_health_monitor()
  with a new _health_monitor_lock so concurrent /step requests cannot
  race to spawn duplicate monitor threads
- Replace assert-inside-try/except with explicit runtime check in
  _cleanup_all_acp_sessions(); assert is stripped under -O and the
  pattern confused static checkers

All 22 ACP tests still pass.

* fix(server): address 4th Greptile pass — lock stop, precise except, no-op thread

Three fixes from the 4th Greptile review:

1. stop_acp_health_monitor: hold _health_monitor_lock when joining and
   nullifying _health_monitor_thread, consistent with start_acp_health_monitor.

2. terminate_subprocess_sync: catch subprocess.TimeoutExpired instead of
   broad Exception so ProcessLookupError/PermissionError from terminate()
   are not silently redirected to the SIGKILL path.

3. _cleanup_all_acp_sessions: null out session.acp_runtime before calling
   remove_session() so it does not spawn a redundant _close_acp_runtime_bg
   thread for an already-terminated process.

All 22 ACP tests still pass.

* fix(server): null acp_runtime before remove_session in health check to prevent spurious close thread

* fix(server): bind acp_runtime to local var in _run_health_check to prevent race

* fix(server): log warning on join timeout + document clean_inactive_sessions scope

* fix(server): move _health_monitor_stop.set() inside lock + snapshot acp_runtime in cleanup

Two concurrency fixes flagged by Greptile eighth pass:

1. Lock gap in stop_acp_health_monitor: _health_monitor_stop.set() was called
   before acquiring _health_monitor_lock, allowing a concurrent
   start_acp_health_monitor() to clear the stop event and leave the old
   monitor thread running after stop() returns. Moving the set() inside
   the lock eliminates this window.

2. TOCTOU in _cleanup_all_acp_sessions: session.acp_runtime was read twice
   without snapshotting. A concurrent thread setting session.acp_runtime = None
   between the None-check and the terminate_subprocess_sync() call would produce
   a spurious AttributeError/warning. Snapshot to a local variable first.

* Update gptme/server/api_v2_sessions.py

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(doctor): add version check and Playwright browser check (#1612)

* feat(doctor): add version check and Playwright browser check

Adds two new diagnostic checks to `gptme-doctor`:

1. **Version check**: Compares installed version against PyPI latest.
   Shows warning for minor/major updates, OK for patch updates.
   Gracefully handles dev installs (skips PyPI) and network errors.

2. **Playwright browser check**: When the browser extra is installed,
   verifies that Playwright browsers are actually available. Common
   gotcha: users install the Python package but forget `playwright
   install chromium`.

Includes 8 new tests covering both checks.

* fix(doctor): guard against current>latest and fix macOS Playwright path

Two bugs found in Greptile review:
- Version check: guard against current >= latest before computing is_major/is_minor.
  If user is running a newer version than PyPI (pre-release, branch install), show OK
  "ahead of PyPI" instead of potentially spurious WARNING.
- Browser check: use platform-specific default path for Playwright browsers.
  macOS uses ~/Library/Caches/ms-playwright, not ~/.cache/ms-playwright.

Also adds a test case for the current>latest scenario.

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(eval): expand basic suite with 4 practical agent capability tests (#1607)

* test(eval): add 4 new eval tests for data processing, refactoring, testing, and debugging

Add practical eval tests that exercise real-world agent capabilities:

- json-transform: Parse JSON, compute derived values, output structured result
- multi-file-refactor: Rename function across multiple files while preserving behavior
- write-tests: Given library code, write comprehensive pytest tests
- fix-import-error: Trace and fix dependency chain by creating missing module

These expand the basic suite from 6 to 10 tests, covering data processing,
multi-file operations, test writing, and dependency debugging - all common
agent workflows not previously tested.

* fix(eval): use substring match in check_refactor_output

'150' in ctx.stdout.split() uses list membership, but python outputs '150.0'
(float), so the check always fails. Use substring matching instead, consistent
with check_read_modify_output.

* Update gptme/eval/suites/basic.py

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eval): restore check_fix_import_output def, validate products list in json-transform check

- Fix syntax error: check_fix_import_output function definition was missing, causing lint failure
- Add products list validation to check_json_transform_output (addresses Greptile review)
- check_fix_import_output already uses substring matching (consistent with check_refactor_output fix)

* fix(eval): address greptile review — anchor calcTotal check, add function coverage check

- check_refactor_main: use "calcTotal(" not in content to avoid false-negatives
  when agent adds comments referencing the old name (e.g. "# was calcTotal")
  Consistent with check_refactor_utils which already anchors to "def calcTotal"

- check_tests_output: replace redundant "passed" check (always true when
  check_tests_pass passes) with function-coverage check that verifies all
  three library functions (factorial, is_palindrome, clamp) appear in the
  verbose pytest -v output, making it a meaningful independent assertion

* fix(eval): address greptile review — exact match for import output, file-based test coverage check

* fix(eval): address final greptile review — import check + math_ops creation check

- check_refactor_main: add import statement check; previously only checked
  call sites (calcTotal() not in content), missing the import line
  (from utils import calcTotal). An agent updating only the call but not
  the import would pass. Now requires "from utils import calculate_total"
  in content.
- fix-import-error: add check_fix_import_created to verify math_ops.py was
  actually created. An agent could have inlined the multiply logic into
  helpers.py and bypassed the module-creation requirement while still
  producing correct output and exit code.

* fix(eval): remove redundant calculate_total check in check_refactor_main

commit.message:
feat(lessons): Thompson sampling effectiveness scoring for hybrid matcher (#1573)

* feat(lessons): wire Thompson sampling into hybrid matcher effectiveness score

Replace the hardcoded 0.5 effectiveness_score in HybridLessonMatcher with
Thompson sampling posterior means loaded from a JSON state file.

When `effectiveness_state_file` is set in HybridConfig, the matcher reads
Beta-Bernoulli posteriors (alpha/(alpha+beta)) and uses them to rank lessons
by empirical effectiveness. Falls back to 0.5 if no data is available.

This enables adaptive lesson selection: lessons that correlate with
productive sessions get higher scores, while ineffective ones are
naturally deprioritized.

Ref: ErikBjare/bob#361

* fix(lessons): address Greptile review feedback on TS posteriors

- Broaden exception handler to catch TypeError and ValueError (non-numeric alpha/beta)
- Use safe division (total > 0 check) to handle zero-count arms
- Explicit float() conversion to catch type errors early
- Use pytest tmp_path fixture instead of manual NamedTemporaryFile cleanup
  to prevent file leaks if assertions fail

* test(lessons): cover TS posterior edge cases and short-path matching

* fix(lessons): per-arm error handling in _load_ts_posteriors, cover basename lookup in test

- Move try/except inside the loop so a single malformed arm is skipped
  without discarding all valid posteriors (fixes Greptile issue #2)
- Catch AttributeError in per-arm handler so non-dict arm values (e.g.
  scalars) are skipped cleanly instead of crashing (fixes Greptile issue #1)
- Outer try/except now covers only JSON/IO errors (JSONDecodeError, OSError)
- test_effectiveness_score_with_ts now calls _effectiveness_score(lesson)
  directly to cover the basename lookup path (fixes Greptile issue #3)
- Add test_load_ts_posteriors_non_dict_arm_skipped to verify AttributeError
  handling: bad arm skipped, valid arms still returned

* fix(lessons): update stale comment and cover _effectiveness_score default fallback in test

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(subagent): add ACP execution mode for multi-harness subagents (#1563)

* feat(subagent): add ACP execution mode for multi-harness subagents

Add `use_acp=True` parameter to subagent() that runs subagents via the
Agent Client Protocol instead of subprocess or thread. This enables
delegating work to any ACP-compatible agent (gptme, Claude Code, Cursor,
etc.) through a standard protocol.

Implementation:
- New `use_acp` and `acp_command` params on subagent() and subagent_batch()
- ACP path runs GptmeAcpClient in a wrapper thread (async-to-sync bridge)
- Collects streamed text from session_update notifications
- Stores results in _subagent_results for standard status/wait flow
- Hook-based completion notifications work identically to other modes

Tests: 3 new tests covering creation, result storage, and error handling.
All 49 subagent tests pass.

Refs: #1534

* fix(subagent): address Greptile review feedback on ACP mode

- Fix race condition: append Subagent to _subagents before starting
  the thread, so the finally block can always find it even on fast
  completion
- Add timeout warning for ACP mode: log when thread is still alive
  after join(timeout=...) returns, making the no-op visible
- Add warnings for unsupported parameters (model, output_schema,
  context_mode, context_include) when use_acp=True

* fix(subagent): address second Greptile review — test thread join + use_subprocess conflict warning

- test_acp_mode_creates_subagent: join thread inside patch context and also
  patch notify_completion, so the daemon thread doesn't outlive the mock and
  accidentally invoke the real GptmeAcpClient after context exit
- subagent.py: emit logger.warning() when both use_acp=True and use_subprocess=True
  are passed simultaneously, making it explicit that use_subprocess is ignored

* fix(subagent): address third Greptile review — asyncio.run, exc_info, batch test

- Replace manual asyncio.new_event_loop()/run_until_complete()/close()
  with asyncio.run() (idiomatic Python 3.10+, cleaner lifecycle)
- Add exc_info=True to ACP exception logger.error() so stack traces
  are preserved and ACP failures are diagnosable
- Add test_acp_mode_subagent_batch to exercise use_acp + acp_command
  forwarding through subagent_batch(), covering the previously
  untested batch path

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(context): show metadata for binary files instead of silently ignoring (#1601)

* feat(context): show metadata for binary files instead of silently ignoring them

When a user passes a binary file path to gptme, instead of silently
returning nothing, now returns a metadata codeblock with:
- File name and size (human-readable)
- MIME type (from mimetypes module)
- Detailed file type info (from `file` command when available)

This helps the LLM understand what the file is even when it can't
read the contents directly.

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

* fix(context): preserve image/PDF handling via msg.files in binary metadata path

When _resource_to_codeblock encounters a binary file via UnicodeDecodeError,
it now returns None for visual formats (png, jpg, jpeg, gif, webp, pdf) instead
of returning a metadata codeblock. This restores the flow into the else branch
of include_paths(), which calls _parse_prompt_files() to add the file to
msg.files for proper visual rendering.

Fixes regression where images were downgraded to text metadata instead of
being passed as visual content. Reported by Greptile review.

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add 4 tests for the completion generation function

commit.message:
feat(tauri): first-run setup wizard for new users (#1596)

* feat(webui): add first-run setup wizard for new users

Adds a guided setup experience shown on first launch:
- Welcome screen introducing gptme capabilities
- Mode selection: Local (self-hosted) vs Cloud (gptme.ai)
- Local mode: auto-detects Tauri desktop (sidecar) vs web (manual server)
- Cloud mode: opens gptme.ai auth flow with deep-link support
- Completion state persisted in settings to show only once

Part of the convergent local + managed service app vision.

Closes gptme/gptme#1593

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

* fix(webui): skip setup wizard in E2E tests via storageState

The first-run wizard blocks E2E tests since they run in a fresh browser.
Pre-set hasCompletedSetup in localStorage via Playwright's storageState
config so the wizard doesn't appear during tests.

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

* fix(settings): treat existing users as having completed setup

Existing users with saved settings but no hasCompletedSetup key
would incorrectly see the wizard. Default to true when the key
is missing from stored settings.

Co-Authored-By: greptile-apps[bot] <190245938+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(webui): address Greptile review — correct pipx command, fix redundant connect(), preserve hasCompletedSetup on reset

* fix(settings): preserve hasCompletedSetup across settings reset

Write the preserved flag to localStorage (not just memory) so it
survives page refreshes after a reset.

* fix(settings): initialize from localStorage synchronously to prevent wizard flash

The setup wizard briefly flashed on initial render because SettingsContext
initialized `hasCompletedSetup` to `false` (default) and only loaded the
saved value from localStorage in a `useEffect` (after first paint).

This caused the Dialog's focus trap to fire for one frame before the
useEffect set `hasCompletedSetup: true`, disrupting keyboard event routing
in the e2e test ("should be able to create a new conversation and send a
message").

Fix: use `useState(loadSettingsFromStorage)` (lazy initializer) to read
localStorage synchronously during first render — no flash, no transient
dialog open.

* fix(webui): address Greptile review — error feedback, stale closure, URL comment

- Show connection error message on local setup step when connect() fails
  (instead of silently advancing to complete step)
- Use functional setSettings updater in resetSettings to avoid stale closure
- Add comment clarifying fleet.gptme.ai is the gptme cloud service URL

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): persist hasCompletedSetup when transitioning to complete step

Call completeSetup() alongside setStep('complete') in both handleLocalSetup
and handleCloudLogin. Previously, if a user reached the 'complete' step via
successful local connection or cloud auth, then refreshed the page before
clicking 'Start using gptme', the wizard would reappear from the beginning.

Addresses Greptile feedback (latest review).

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): use local isOpen state so complete step is reachable

React 18 batches completeSetup() + setStep('complete') into one render,
which caused the early-return guard (if hasCompletedSetup return null) to
fire before the complete step could render — making 'You're all set!'
unreachable.

Fix: replace early-return + Dialog open binding with local isOpen state.
The dialog stays open until closeWizard() is called (Skip / Start button),
while completeSetup() is called eagerly on transition to 'complete' so
a refresh before clicking 'Start using gptme' won't re-show the wizard.

Also: fix stale closure in updateSettings (functional updater), and add
a comment to playwright.config.ts about overriding storageState for
wizard-specific E2E tests.

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): clear connectError when navigating Back from local step

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): address final Greptile feedback

- SettingsContext: restore console.error in loadSettingsFromStorage catch
  (was silently swallowing parse errors, inconsistent with other handlers)
- SetupWizard: allow Escape key to close wizard (ARIA dialog accessibility)
  — onEscapeKeyDown now calls closeWizard() instead of preventDefault()
- SetupWizard: document isOpen one-time-snapshot semantics in comment
  (intentional design: wizard manages its own lifecycle after mount)

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <190245938+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

and handleCloudLogin. Previously, if a user reached the 'complete' step via
successful local connection or cloud auth, then refreshed the page before
clicking 'Start using gptme', the wizard would reappear from the beginning.

Addresses Greptile feedback (latest review).

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): use local isOpen state so complete step is reachable

React 18 batches completeSetup() + setStep('complete') into one render,
which caused the early-return guard (if hasCompletedSetup return null) to
fire before the complete step could render — making 'You're all set!'
unreachable.

Fix: replace early-return + Dialog open binding with local isOpen state.
The dialog stays open until closeWizard() is called (Skip / Start button),
while completeSetup() is called eagerly on transition to 'complete' so
a refresh before clicking 'Start using gptme' won't re-show the wizard.

Also: fix stale closure in updateSettings (functional updater), and add
a comment to playwright.config.ts about overriding storageState for
wizard-specific E2E tests.

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): clear connectError when navigating Back from local step

* fix(webui/SetupWizard): address final Greptile feedback

- SettingsContext: restore console.error in loadSettingsFromStorage catch
  (was silently swallowing parse errors, inconsistent with other handlers)
- SetupWizard: allow Escape key to close wizard (ARIA dialog accessibility)
  — onEscapeKeyDown now calls closeWizard() instead of preventDefault()
- SetupWizard: document isOpen one-time-snapshot semantics in comment
  (intentional design: wizard manages its own lifecycle after mount)

commit.message:
feat(server): add GPTME_USE_ACP_DEFAULT config for server-side ACP opt-in (#1580)

* feat(server): add GPTME_USE_ACP_DEFAULT config for server-side ACP opt-in

* fix(server): read GPTME_USE_ACP_DEFAULT from os.environ directly

Addresses Greptile review feedback:

1. Avoid per-request LRU cache-clear: `_get_use_acp_default` was calling
   `Config.from_workspace()` which unconditionally clears the shared
   `_get_project_config_cached` LRU cache as a side-effect. Since
   `GPTME_USE_ACP_DEFAULT` is a process-level env var, read `os.environ`
   directly instead.

2. Remove redundant `or False`: `get_env_bool(..., default=False)` already
   returns `bool`; the trailing `or False` was dead code.

3. Isolate ambient env in test: add `monkeypatch.delenv` guards in
   `test_without_env_var_default_remains_false` so the test is stable
   even when `GPTME_USE_ACP_DEFAULT` is set in the developer's shell or CI.

---------

commit.message: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/feat/tauri-subtree-merge'

fix(lessons): deduplicate same-named lessons across directories (#1594)

* fix(lessons): deduplicate same-named lessons across directories

When multiple lesson directories are configured (e.g. workspace lessons/
and gptme-contrib/lessons/), lessons with the same relative filename but
different physical files were both loaded, causing double-injection.

Add filename-based deduplication alongside the existing realpath-based
dedup. The relative path from each directory root is tracked, and the
first directory in config order wins. This handles the common case where
an agent workspace has locally-modified copies of lessons that also
exist in gptme-contrib/lessons/.

Closes #1589

* fix(lessons): address Greptile review feedback

- Rename seen_filenames → seen_rel_paths (stores relative paths, not bare filenames)
- Move seen_paths/seen_rel_paths.add() before status check to enforce strict
  'first directory wins' semantics regardless of lesson active status
- Add test: inactive lesson in earlier dir suppresses active copy in later dir

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(test): increase timeout for test_create_template_mode_e2e to 120s

The test clones from GitHub and regularly takes 59-65s in CI.
With the default 60s timeout it hits the limit, gets retried, and
the retry triggers a pytest-retry + tmp_path stash interaction bug
(KeyError in _pytest.stash teardown).

Give the test an explicit 120s timeout so it completes without retry.

commit.message:
fix(ci): add placeholder icons to build/release jobs before icon generation

The tauri::generate_context! proc macro panics at compile time if icon files
are missing. The lint job already creates placeholder icons, but the build
and release jobs only run `tauri icon || true` which silently fails when icon
generation tools are unavailable. Add the same placeholder creation step
before the icon generation attempt so Rust compilation succeeds even when
`tauri icon` fails.

commit.message:
feat(eval): add Terminal-Bench (tbench) evaluation module (#1582)

* feat(eval): add Terminal-Bench (tbench) evaluation module

Implements tbench support in gptme core, similar to the existing
SWE-bench integration. This allows running gptme against the
Terminal-Bench benchmark harness.

Changes:
- `gptme/eval/tbench/agent.py`: GptmeAgent implementing AbstractInstalledAgent,
  wraps gptme -n for use in terminal-bench's evaluation harness
- `gptme/eval/tbench/setup.sh`: Docker container setup script, installs gptme
  from latest master into an isolated venv (Ubuntu 24.04 compatible)
- `gptme/eval/tbench/run.py`: gptme-eval-tbench CLI, wraps tb run with
  gptme pre-configured as the agent
- `gptme/eval/tbench/__init__.py`: Module exports
- `pyproject.toml`: gptme-eval-tbench entry point + terminal-bench optional dep
  (python>=3.12 marker, matching tbench's own requirement)

Usage:
  # Install terminal-bench
  uv tool install terminal-bench

  # Run via CLI
  gptme-eval-tbench --task hello-world --model anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5

  # Or use terminal-bench directly
  tb run --dataset terminal-bench-core==head \
      --agent-import-path gptme.eval.tbench.agent:GptmeAgent \
      --task-id hello-world

Resolves: ErikBjare/bob#532 (tbench support in gptme core)

* fix(tbench): address review concerns — timeout, shlex, reproducibility, curl|sh

- agent.py: replace float("inf") timeout with 600s default, configurable
  via max_timeout_sec constructor arg (prevents silent benchmark hangs)
- run.py: use shlex.join() instead of ' '.join() so JSON agent-args
  are properly quoted in the displayed command
- setup.sh: add GPTME_VERSION env var (defaults to "master") so callers
  can pin to a tag/commit for reproducible benchmark runs
- setup.sh: install uv via pip instead of curl|sh (avoids piped-curl
  security concern; removes curl system dependency)

* fix(tbench): address second Greptile review — packaging, lazy import, error messages

- pyproject.toml: add setup.sh to include directive so it ships in wheels
- __init__.py: lazy import GptmeAgent to prevent ImportError traceback when
  terminal-bench is not installed (lets run.py's friendly error message fire)
- run.py: separate FileNotFoundError from CalledProcessError to give accurate
  "not installed" vs "installed but broken" diagnostics

* fix(tbench): add --break-system-packages for pip on Ubuntu 24.04

* fix(tbench): install uv inside venv instead of using --break-system-packages

Addresses Erik's feedback: using --break-system-packages is risky if the
script is accidentally run outside a container. Instead, create the venv
first and install uv inside it — no system packages are ever modified.

Before:
  python3 -m pip install --break-system-packages uv  # dangerous
  python3 -m venv /opt/gptme-venv

After:
  python3 -m venv /opt/gptme-venv
  /opt/gptme-venv/bin/pip install uv  # isolated, safe

* fix(tbench): fix install instructions and add dataset reproducibility note

- Replace 'uv tool install terminal-bench' with 'pip install gptme[eval]'
  in docstrings and error messages — uv tool creates an isolated venv where
  gptme is not present, causing ImportError when tb tries to load the agent
- Add noqa: PLC0415 to lazy import in __init__.py to silence lint warning
- Improve --dataset help text to mention pinning for reproducible benchmarks

* fix(eval/tbench): update stale install instruction in agent.py ImportError

* fix(eval/tbench): share DEFAULT_MODEL constant + add strict shell options

- Extract `DEFAULT_MODEL = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` into
  `__init__.py` and import it in both `agent.py` and `run.py` so
  the two files can't silently drift to different defaults (per
  Erik's review comment).
- Add `set -euo pipefail` to `setup.sh` for standard defensive
  shell options (pipefail + nounset).

* fix(eval/tbench): use PROVIDER_API_KEYS from gptme.llm for all providers

Extracts the provider→env_var mapping from list_available_providers() into
a module-level PROVIDER_API_KEYS constant in gptme/llm/__init__.py. This is
the single source of truth Erik suggested — in gptme/llm/ rather than
scattered across modules.

The _env property in GptmeAgent now imports and iterates PROVIDER_API_KEYS,
covering all 8 providers (openai, anthropic, openrouter, gemini, groq, xai,
deepseek, azure) instead of only the original 4.

Addresses: https://github.com/gptme/gptme/pull/1582#issuecomment-2878185332

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(lessons): inject keyword count instead of text to prevent self-referential corpus matches (#1581)

* fix(lessons): inject keyword count instead of text to prevent self-referential corpus matches

When lessons are injected with 'Matched by: <keyword-text>', those keyword
phrases appear verbatim in session transcripts. This causes lesson effectiveness
analysis (keyword corpus simulation) to count self-referential matches —
the lesson's own injected header triggers the keyword on every future trajectory
that passes through that session's transcript.

Replace the keyword text with a count only:
  Before: *Matched by: read ALL review sources without truncation, ...*
  After:  *Matched by: 2 keyword(s)*

The Claude Code hook (match-lessons.py) already uses count-only format.
This makes gptme core consistent and eliminates the false-positive signal.

* fix(lessons): apply same keyword-count fix to tools/lessons.py injection path

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(gh): add multi-line comment guidance to prevent literal \n in posts (#1572)

* fix(gh): add multi-line comment guidance to prevent literal \n in posts

When LLMs generate gh issue comment commands with --body "text\nmore text",
bash does not interpret \n as a newline in double-quoted strings, resulting
in literal backslash-n appearing in GitHub comments.

Add explicit instructions to use --body-file with heredoc for multi-line
bodies, and add an example showing the correct pattern. Also add a warning
against the broken --body "...\n..." pattern.

Fixes the root cause of literal \n appearing in comments posted via gptme
when using openai-subscription or other providers.

* fix(gh): also document $variable expansion prevention with single-quoted heredoc

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

The else branch was unreachable dead code. Fix by checking type(auto_confirm) is bool

commit.message:
feat(server): add ACP session runtime wrapper for per-session subprocesses (#1546)

* feat(server): add ACP session runtime wrapper for per-session subprocesses

* fix(tests): skip acp_session_runtime tests when server extras not installed

* fix(server): cleanup ACP client subprocess on new_session() failure to prevent process leak

* feat(server): wire AcpSessionRuntime into server session step routing

When a step request includes `use_acp: true`, the conversation session
is backed by an ACP subprocess (via AcpSessionRuntime) for the rest of
its lifetime. Subsequent steps are routed through the subprocess rather
than running the LLM in-process.

Changes:
- ConversationSession: add use_acp + acp_runtime fields
- api_conversation_step: create AcpSessionRuntime on first use_acp=True step
- _acp_step(): async step handler — prompts the ACP runtime, emits SSE events
  (generation_started + generation_complete), persists the assistant message
- _start_acp_step_thread(): launches _acp_step in a background thread via asyncio.run
- _close_acp_runtime_bg(): closes ACP runtime safely in a background thread on session removal
- StepRequest: document the new use_acp field in OpenAPI schema
- Tests: 2 new integration tests (direct _acp_step event emission + HTTP step routing)

Known limitations (intentional for this evaluation PR):
- No per-token streaming (ACP returns the full response at once)
- Tool confirmations are auto-approved inside the subprocess
- No STEP_PRE / TURN_POST server hooks when using ACP mode

* fix(server): avoid duplicate ACP steps without new user input

* fix(server): process all pending ACP user turns per step

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

* feat(server): run STEP_PRE/TURN_POST hooks in ACP session mode

* feat(server): align ACP session runtime model with step request

* feat(server): bridge ACP session updates to SSE token stream

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: automated demo capture pipeline (asciinema + screenshots + recordings) (#1558)

* feat: add automated demo capture pipeline

Add script and CI workflow for capturing gptme demos in three modes:

1. Terminal recordings via asciinema (CLI demo scenarios)
2. WebUI screenshots via Playwright (desktop + mobile viewports)
3. WebUI screen recordings via Playwright video capture

The script (scripts/demo_capture.py) handles server lifecycle,
prerequisite checking, and generates a summary JSON of captured assets.

CI workflow runs monthly or on-demand via workflow_dispatch with
mode selection. Assets are uploaded as GitHub Actions artifacts.

Closes #1554

* fix(demo): cast dict values to str for Playwright API calls

Fixes mypy errors on Python 3.10 where dict value lookups
return Collection[str] instead of str. The wait_for_selector()
and click() APIs expect str arguments.

* fix(demo): handle TimeoutExpired in server cleanup finally block

* feat(demo): add --model flag to pass through to gptme

* feat(demo): add --timeout flag and increase default to 180s

Complex demos (multi-file creation + tests) need more than 120s.
Default timeout increased from 120s to 180s, with --timeout CLI flag
for flexibility.

* fix(demo): avoid pytest dependency in file-editing demo

The previous prompt "write tests and run them" caused gptme to
try installing pytest via sudo, which hangs in non-interactive
asciinema recording. Changed to explicit "python3 test_calc.py"
with assert statements instead.

- Fix pre/post-click wait selectors for conversation screenshots
- Add scroll_percent option for positioning within long conversations
- Add post_click_wait for separate pre-click and post-click selectors
- Use JS-based scrolling to find scrollable containers
- Capture 3 screenshots: home, mobile, conversation with code/tools

commit.message:
fix(codeblock): prevent premature block extraction in streaming mode (#1564)

When streaming content line-by-line, split("\n") on content ending with
"\n" produces a trailing empty string that is indistinguishable from a
real blank line. The streaming parser used this empty string as block
closure confirmation, causing premature extraction when the closing
fence was the last content received.

Fix: in streaming mode, ignore a blank line that is the very last element
of the split array, since it's a split artifact rather than a real
confirmation line. A real blank line will have more content after it
(or at minimum, another empty string from a second "\n").

Also updates the patch codeblock test to explicitly add a trailing blank
line (since end-of-file-fixer strips it from the data file).

Removes the xfail from test_save_with_bare_backticks_incremental_streaming
which demonstrated the issue with incremental streaming of nested codeblocks.

Ref: PR #1429

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(context): use plain git status instead of -vv (#1561)

* fix(context): use plain `git status` instead of `-vv` in context generation

`git status -vv` dumps full diffs of both staged and unstaged changes into
the system prompt. In workspaces with large data files, this can produce
hundreds of KB of output (409KB in one case), causing "Prompt is too long"
failures that block autonomous agent runs for hours.

Plain `git status` provides the file list which is sufficient context —
agents can always read specific diffs when needed.

Reported-by: Gordon (agent)

* fix(context): add truncation limit and update TODO comment

Address review feedback:
- Add 10k char truncation to git_status() as a safety bound
- Update TODO comment in prompts.py to match (no longer -vv)

---------

commit.message:
ci(bot): only run when comment mentions @gptme (#1560)

Without this filter, CI spins up on every issue/PR comment to immediately
fail the allowlist check — wasting runner minutes for nothing.

Discovered in ErikBjare/bob where ~26 runs/day were caused by the bot
workflow triggering on all comments (greptile reviews, CI notifications,
user discussion, etc.) when there was nothing to do.

Fix: add an `if` condition that skips the job unless the comment body
contains '@gptme'. Matches the standard bot interaction pattern since users
must @-mention the bot to trigger it anyway.

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(eval): add fix-bug and read-modify eval tests

Add two new eval tests to the basic suite that test more realistic
coding tasks:

- fix-bug: Given buggy fibonacci code with infinite recursion, the
  agent must read, diagnose, and fix the bug

  back to package version when not in a git repo, fixing the TODO

  crashes

* fix(eval): use word-boundary matching in check_read_modify_output

Prevent false positive where '5' in output is satisfied by '50' (the max
value). Use split() for count and max checks, keep substring match for
mean to handle both '30' and '30.0' float output.

feat(release): generate structured changelogs for all release types (#1550)

* feat(release): generate structured changelogs for all release types

The scheduled-release CI workflow and Makefile release targets now use
build_changelog.py to produce categorized changelogs (Features, Fixes,
Contributors) instead of GitHub's flat auto-generated notes.

Range selection: dev releases use since-last-tag (incremental), stable
releases use since-last-stable (cumulative).

Closes #1532

* fix(release): use stable-only range for stable release changelogs in Makefile

finding the previous version. This ensures stable release changelogs are
cumulative (e.g. v0.32.0 shows all changes since v0.31.0, not just since
the last dev release like v0.31.1.dev20260227).

Dev releases continue to use the immediately preceding tag (incremental behavior).

Addresses Greptile review finding on PR #1550.

commit.message:
feat(hooks): add CWD_CHANGED hook type for directory change events (#1539)

* feat(hooks): add CWD_CHANGED hook type for directory change events

Adds a centralized CWD_CHANGED hook type that fires when the working
directory changes during tool execution, replacing the duplicated
pre/post CWD comparison pattern in cwd_tracking.py and agents_md_inject.py.

Changes:
- Add HookType.CWD_CHANGED enum value and CwdChangedHook Protocol
- Add cwd_changed.py: centralized detector (TOOL_EXECUTE_PRE/POST)
  that stores CWD before execution and triggers CWD_CHANGED if it changed
- Simplify cwd_tracking.py: subscribes to CWD_CHANGED, just yields notification
- Simplify agents_md_inject.py: subscribes to CWD_CHANGED, removes own pre/post
- Add 23 tests covering detector, subscribers, and integration

Closes #1521

* fix(tests): update hook tests for CWD_CHANGED refactoring

The cwd_tracking and agents_md_inject hooks were refactored in the same PR
to subscribe to HookType.CWD_CHANGED instead of using their own pre/post
ContextVar tracking. The in-package tests still imported the removed symbols
(_cwd_before_var, pre_execute, post_execute, track_cwd_pre_execute,
track_cwd_post_execute), causing 6 mypy errors and 2 collection errors.

Updated both test files to use the new on_cwd_changed() API that takes
(log, workspace, old_cwd, new_cwd, tool_use) directly.

* refactor(hooks): rename cwd_tracking to cwd_awareness

Following the naming convention of time_awareness, token_awareness,
cost_awareness etc. The module injects awareness hints about CWD changes
rather than tracking changes itself — "awareness" better reflects its role.

- Rename cwd_tracking.py → cwd_awareness.py
- Rename test_cwd_tracking.py → test_cwd_awareness.py
- Update hook registration name: cwd_tracking.notification → cwd_awareness.notification

commit.message:
ci: add scheduled release workflow (#1537)

* ci: add scheduled release workflow for automated dev/stable releases

Add automated release pipeline with:
- Twice-weekly cron (Mon + Thu at noon UTC) for dev pre-releases
- Manual workflow_dispatch for dev, patch, or minor releases
- Pre-flight checks: skips if no new commits, verifies CI green on HEAD
- PEP 440-compliant versioning with .devYYYYMMDD suffix
- Same-day collision handling with incrementing suffix
- Concurrency group to prevent race conditions

The build.yml tag matching is also fixed to use startsWith() instead
of equality check with wildcard.

PyPI publishing is handled by the existing release.yml workflow
which triggers on GitHub release creation.

* refactor(release): unify bump_version.sh for both interactive and CI use

Merge bump_version.sh (interactive) and bump_version_auto.sh (CI) into
a single script with two modes:

- No flags: Interactive mode for `make release` (prompts for version,
  validates working tree, creates signed tags)
- --type dev|patch|minor: Automated mode for CI and `make version-auto`
  (computes version from last stable tag, creates unsigned tags)

Both modes share tag discovery, version computation, pyproject.toml
updates, and metadata output ($GITHUB_OUTPUT support).

This addresses Erik's feedback to share code between `make release` and
the scheduled-release CI workflow.

* fix(ci): address Greptile review — inline PyPI publish, force master checkout

- Inline PyPI publishing in this workflow (GITHUB_TOKEN can't trigger
  release.yml due to GitHub's anti-recursion policy; must publish inline)
- Add `ref: master` to release job checkout to prevent manual dispatch
  from a non-master branch accidentally pushing that branch to master

* ci(scheduled-release): remove unused id-token permission

* refactor(release): extract shared publish_release.sh, add Makefile targets

Extract release publishing logic (push, GH release, PyPI publish) into a
shared `scripts/publish_release.sh` used by both `make release` and the
scheduled-release CI workflow. This addresses Erik's request to share
code/scripts between the CI workflow and `make release`.

Changes:
- New `scripts/publish_release.sh`: shared push + GH release + PyPI publish
  - Supports `--notes-file` for structured changelogs (make release)
  - Supports `--publish-pypi` for PyPI publishing (CI, make release-patch)
  - Supports `--dry-run` for testing
- Makefile: add `release-dev`, `release-patch`, `release-minor` targets
  that mirror what CI does, for local testing
- Makefile: `make release` now uses publish_release.sh for push + GH release
- CI workflow: simplified from 3 steps (push, GH release, PyPI) to 1 step
  calling the shared script
- Removed unused `id-token: write` permission (Greptile feedback)
- Removed unused job output forwarding (last_stable, last_tag, commits)

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(acp): add GptmeAcpClient — gptme as ACP client (#1536)

* feat(acp): add GptmeAcpClient for spawning ACP agents as subprocesses

Implements gptme as an ACP *client* — the counterpart to the existing
gptme-acp agent (server) mode.

Key additions:
- `gptme/acp/client.py`: `GptmeAcpClient` async context manager that
  uses `acp.stdio.spawn_agent_process` to launch any ACP-compatible
  agent (default: `gptme-acp`) as a subprocess with an isolated cwd.
  Includes `_MinimalClient` callback handler (auto-confirm permissions,
  optional update callback) and `acp_client()` shorthand helper.
- `tests/test_acp_client.py`: 18 unit tests covering `_MinimalClient`
  callbacks, `GptmeAcpClient` interface/error paths, and module exports.

Primary motivation: the HTTP server (`api_v2_sessions.py`) currently
does a process-wide `os.chdir(workspace)` to set session context (see
#1486). Spawning one `gptme-acp` process per session via this client
gives each session its own cwd without global-state side effects.

Closes #1534 (first step — server integration is the next PR).

* fix(acp): add type: ignore[import-not-found] for optional acp imports, type kwargs, cleanup

- Add # type: ignore[import-not-found] to all acp/acp.schema/acp.stdio imports
  in both client.py and test_acp_client.py (matches pattern from agent.py)
- Remove unused _acp_available variable
- Remove redundant _check_acp() call from __init__ (still called in __aenter__)
- Fix docstring example: result.stop_reason (not result.output_text)
- Type the kwargs in acp_client() instead of **kwargs: Any

* fix(typecheck): add mypy override for acp.* optional import

The acp package is optional (extras: acp). Add it to mypy overrides
so the module is treated consistently with other optional deps
(opentelemetry, langchain, numpy, etc.) instead of relying on
inline type: ignore comments.

* fix(acp): support async session update callbacks

Co-authored-by: Bob <bob@superuserlabs.org>

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tools): support loading custom tools from .py files via --tools (#1540)

* feat(tools): support loading custom tools from .py files via --tools flag

The --tools flag now accepts .py file paths alongside tool names.
This enables users to load custom ToolSpec definitions without
packaging them as plugins.

Examples:
  gptme --tools shell,path/to/mytool.py  # built-in + custom
  gptme --tools ./my_tool.py             # custom tool only

Also fixes load_from_file to use inspect.getmembers for proper
ToolSpec discovery (previously relied on comparing loaded tools
before/after import, which didn't work correctly).

* chore: remove stale TODO comments

- prompt.py: Remove contradictory TODO about cache (lru_cache already present)
- todo.py: Update stale TODO about persistence (auto-replay already handles it)
- cli/main.py: Remove commented-out test conversation filter (already in feature commit)

* fix(tools): use spec_from_file_location to avoid module name collisions

When loading multiple .py files with the same filename from different
directories, importlib.import_module(stem) would hit the sys.modules cache
and return the first loaded module instead of the new file.

Fix by using importlib.util.spec_from_file_location with a unique module
name based on the full path hash, ensuring each file gets its own module.

Also removes the sys.path manipulation that was needed for import_module
but is no longer required with spec_from_file_location.

Adds test_load_from_file_collision to verify the fix.

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(auto-naming): consolidate duplicated auto-naming logic (#1542)

* refactor(auto-naming): consolidate duplicated auto-naming logic into try_auto_name

Both chat.py (CLI) and api_v2_sessions.py (server) had duplicated logic for
auto-generating conversation names: checking assistant message count, calling
auto_generate_display_name, updating config, saving, and error handling.

Extract this into a shared try_auto_name() function in util/auto_naming.py.
CLI still runs it in a background thread; server calls it synchronously and
adds SSE notification. Resolves the TODO comments in both files.

* perf(chat): avoid spawning naming threads after auto-naming window closes

Pre-check assistant_count and config.name in chat.py before spawning
a thread + doing disk I/O (ChatConfig.from_logdir) + deepcopy of
messages. After the naming window (> MAX_ASSISTANT_MSGS_FOR_NAMING),
none of those operations are needed.

Exports MAX_ASSISTANT_MSGS_FOR_NAMING from auto_naming.py so both
callers share the same constant.

Addresses Greptile review suggestion.

---------

Co-authored-by: TimeToBuildBob <TimeToBuildBob@users.noreply.github.com>
